sections.label: Section 171b
sections.original: οὗτος δʼ , ὦ γεραιέ , τίς κυρεῖ ,
sections.translation: Who is that , old man , on his chariot , driving white horses ?

sections.label: Section 173
sections.original: ὁ μάντις Ἀμφιάραος , ὦ δέσποινʼ , ὅδε ·
sections.translation: That , lady , is the prophet Amphiaraus ; with him are the victims , earth ’ s bloodthirsty streams .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.translation: Daughter of the sun with dazzling zone , O moon , you circle of golden light , how quietly , with what restraint he drives , goading first one horse , then the other ! But where is the one who utters those dreadful insults against this city ?

sections.label: Section 180
sections.translation: Capaneus ? There he is , calculating how he may scale the towers , taking the measure of our walls up and down .

sections.label: Section 182
sections.translation: O Nemesis , and roaring thunder-peals of Zeus and blazing lightning-bolts , oh ! put to sleep his presumptuous boasting !

sections.label: Section 185
sections.translation: This is the man who says he will give the Theban girls as captives of his spear to the women of Mycenae , to Lerna ’ s trident , and the waters of Amymone , dear to Poseidon , when he has them enslaved .

sections.label: Section 190
sections.translation: Never , never , Lady Artemis , golden-haired child of Zeus , may I endure that slavery .

sections.label: Section 193
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ἔσβα δῶμα καὶ κατὰ στέγας
sections.translation: My child , go inside , and stay beneath the shelter of your maiden chamber , now that you have had

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: ἐς τέρψιν ἦλθες ὧν ἔχρῃζες εἰσιδεῖν .
sections.translation: your wish and seen all that you wanted ; for a crowd of women is coming toward the royal palace , as confusion enters the city . Now women by nature love scandal ; and if they get some slight handle for their gossip

sections.label: Section 200
sections.translation: they exaggerate it , for women seem to have pleasure in saying nothing wholesome about each other . Exeunt Antigone and the old servant .

sections.label: Section 1.202
sections.translation: From the Tyrian swell of the sea I came , a choice offering for Loxias from the island of Phoenicia ,

sections.label: Section 1.205
sections.translation: to be a slave to Phoebus in his halls , where he dwells under the snow-swept peaks of Parnassus ; through the Ionian sea I sailed in the waves ,

sections.label: Section 1.210
sections.translation: over the unharvested plains , in the gusts of Zephyrus that ride from Sicily , sweetest music in the sky .

sections.label: Section 1.214
sections.translation: Chosen from my city

sections.label: Section 1.215
sections.translation: as beauty ’ s gift for Loxias , to the land of Cadmus I came , sent here to the towers of Laius , the home of my kin , the famous sons of Agenor .

sections.label: Section 1.220
sections.translation: And I became the handmaid of Phoebus , dedicated like his statues of wrought gold . But the water of Castalia is still waiting for me to drench the maiden glory of my hair

sections.label: Section 1.225
sections.translation: for the service of Phoebus .

sections.label: Section 226
sections.translation: Hail , rock that lights up a double-crested flash of fire above the frenzied heights of Dionysus ; and the vine , that every day

sections.label: Section 230
sections.translation: lets fall the lush cluster of grapes ; and the holy cavern of the serpent and the gods ’ watchtower on the hills , and the sacred snow-swept mountain !

sections.label: Section 235
sections.translation: Would I were free of fear and circling in the dance of the deathless god , having left Dirce for the valleys of Phoebus at the center of the world .

sections.label: Section 2.239
sections.translation: But now I find

sections.label: Section 2.240
sections.translation: the impetuous god of war has come to battle before the walls , and is kindling a murderous blaze — may he not succeed !— for this city . For a friend ’ s pain is shared , and if this land with its seven towers

sections.label: Section 2.245
sections.translation: suffers any mischance , Phoenicia ’ s realm will share it . Ah me ! our blood is one ; we are all children of Io , the horned maid ; these sorrows I claim as mine .

sections.label: Section 2.250
sections.translation: Around the city a thick cloud of shields is kindling a shape of bloody battle , which Ares will soon learn , if he brings upon the sons of Oedipus

sections.label: Section 2.255
sections.translation: the curse of the Furies . O Argos , city of Pelasgia ! I dread your might , and also what comes from the gods ; for the one who approaches his home in armor is setting out to a contest

sections.label: Section 2.260
sections.translation: that is not without justice .

sections.label: Section 261
sections.original: τὰ μὲν πυλωρῶν κλῇθρά μʼ εἰσεδέξατο
sections.translation: The doorkeeper ’ s bolts admitted me readily within the walls , and so I fear that now they have caught me in their nets , they will not let me out unscathed ;

sections.label: Section 265
sections.translation: so I must turn my eye in every direction , here and there , to guard against treachery . Armed with this sword , I shall inspire myself with the confidence that is born of boldness . Starting . Oh ! Who is that ? Or is it a sound I fear ?

sections.label: Section 270
sections.translation: Everything seems a danger to the daring , when their feet begin to tread an enemy ’ s country . Still I trust my mother , and at the same time mistrust her , the one who persuaded me to come here under truce . Well , there is help at hand , for the altar ’ s hearth

sections.label: Section 275
sections.translation: is close and the house is not deserted . Come , let me sheath my sword in its dark scabbard and ask these women standing near the house , who they are .

sections.label: Section 278
sections.original: ξέναι γυναῖκες , εἴπατʼ , ἐκ ποίας πάτρας
sections.translation: Ladies of another land , tell me from what country do you come to the halls of Hellas ?

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: Φοίνισσα μὲν γῆ πατρὶς ἡ θρέψασά με ,
sections.translation: Phoenicia is my native land where I was born and bred ; and the grandsons of Agenor sent me here as first-fruits of the spoil of war for Phoebus . But when the noble son of Oedipus was about to send me to the hallowed oracle and the altars of Loxias ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.translation: the Argive army came against his city . Now tell me in return who you are , who have come to this fortress of the Theban land with its seven gates .

sections.label: Section 288
sections.original: πατὴρ μὲν ἡμῖν Οἰδίπους ὁ Λαΐου ,
sections.translation: My father was Oedipus , the son of Laius ; my mother Jocasta , daughter of Menoeceus ;

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: καλεῖ δὲ Πολυνείκη με Θηβαῖος λεώς .
sections.translation: and I am called Polyneices by the people of Thebes .

sections.label: Section 291
sections.translation: O kinsman of Agenor ’ s race , my royal masters who sent me here !

sections.label: Section 293
sections.translation: I fall to my knees before you , lord , honoring the custom of my home .

sections.label: Section 295
sections.translation: At last you have come to your native land . Hail to you ! all hail ! Lady , come from the house , open wide the gates ! Do you hear , you who gave birth to this man ? Why do you delay to leave the sheltered hall

sections.label: Section 300
sections.translation: and hold your son in your embrace ?

sections.label: Section 301
sections.translation: Maidens , I hear your Phoenician voice , and my old feet drag their tottering steps . O my son ,

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: χρόνῳ σὸν ὄμμα μυρίαις τʼ ἐν ἁμέραις
sections.translation: at last after countless days I see your face ; throw your arms about your mother ’ s breast , stretch out to me your cheeks and the dark , curly locks of your hair , overshadowing my neck .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.translation: Hail to you ! all hail ! scarcely here in your mother ’ s arms , beyond hope and expectation . What can I say to you ? How in every way , by hands , by words , in the mazy delight

sections.label: Section 315
sections.translation: of the dance , shall I find the pleasure of my former joy ? Ah ! my son , you left your father ’ s house desolate , when your brother ’ s outrage drove you away in exile .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.translation: Truly you were missed alike by your friends and Thebes . And so I cut my white hair and let it fall for grief , in tears , not clad in robes of white , my son ,

sections.label: Section 325
sections.translation: but taking instead these dark rags .

sections.label: Section 327
sections.original: ὁ δʼ ἐν δόμοισι πρέσβυς ὀμματοστερὴς
sections.translation: While in the house the old blind man , always possessed by his tearful longing for the pair of brothers estranged from the home ,

sections.label: Section 330
sections.translation: rushed to kill himself with the sword or by the noose suspended over his chamber-roof , moaning his curses on his sons ;

sections.label: Section 335
sections.translation: and now he hides himself in darkness , always weeping and lamenting . And you , my child , I hear you have married and are begetting children to your joy in a foreign home ,

sections.label: Section 340
sections.translation: and are courting a foreign alliance , a ceaseless regret to me your mother and to Laius your ancestor , ruin brought by your marriage . I was not the one who lit for you the marriage-torch ,

sections.label: Section 345
sections.translation: the custom in marriage for a happy mother ; Ismenus had no part at your wedding in supplying the luxurious bath , and there was silence through the streets of Thebes , at the entrance of your bride .

sections.label: Section 350
sections.translation: Curses on them ! whether the sword or strife or your father that is to blame , or heaven ’ s visitation that has burst riotously upon the house of Oedipus ; for on me has come all the anguish of these evils .

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: δεινὸν γυναιξὶν αἱ διʼ ὠδίνων γοναί ,
sections.translation: Their offspring are a wonderful thing to women ; all of them have some love for their children .

sections.label: Section 357
sections.original: μῆτερ , φρονῶν εὖ κοὐ φρονῶν ἀφικόμην
sections.translation: Mother , I have come among enemies wisely or foolishly ; but all men must love their native land ; whoever says otherwise

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: λόγοισι χαίρει τὸν δὲ νοῦν ἐκεῖσʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: is pleased to say so , but his thoughts are turned elsewhere . I was so fearful and in such terror , lest my brother should kill me by treachery , that I came through the city sword in hand , looking all round . I had one advantage ,

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: σπονδαί τε καὶ σὴ πίστις , ἥ μʼ ἐσήγαγε
sections.translation: the truce and your word , which brought me to to the paternal walls ; and I arrived here weeping , to see after a long time my home and the altars of the gods , the training ground , scene of my childhood , and the water of Dirce , from which I was unjustly driven to live in a foreign city ,

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: ναίω , διʼ ὄσσων νᾶμʼ ἔχων δακρύρροον .
sections.translation: a stream of tears flowing from my eyes . Now , grief upon grief , I see you with hair cut short and in black robes , alas for my sorrows !

sections.label: Section 374
sections.original: ὡς δεινὸν ἔχθρα , μῆτερ , οἰκείων φίλων
sections.translation: What a terrible thing , mother , is hatred between dear friends .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.translation: and how hard it makes reconciliation

sections.label: Section 376
sections.original: τί γὰρ πατήρ μοι πρέσβυς ἐν δόμοισι δρᾷ ,
sections.translation: What is my old father doing within the house , looking on darkness ? What of my two sisters ? Surely the unhappy ones lament my exile ?

sections.label: Section 379
sections.original: κακῶς θεῶν τις Οἰδίπου φθείρει γένος ·
sections.translation: Some god with evil intent is destroying the race of Oedipus .
